metadata.description: A small, opinionated set of multi-agent plugins for Claude Code. Bridges to OpenAI Codex and Google Antigravity, a Delphi debate primitive, token-efficient task-grading triage, and the forge lifecycle — sufficient for every capability worth multi-agenting, deliberately lightweight by construction.

plugins.name: codex
plugins.description: OpenAI Codex CLI bridge: hard reasoning (gpt-5.5 xhigh), code review (high), Playwright browser (medium), image generation (gpt-image-2), session resume, raw exec. Structured 5-section handoff spec with audit trail on every call. Default effort is medium; triage escalates per task.

plugins.name: agy
plugins.description: Google Antigravity CLI bridge: 1M-token long-context analysis (Gemini 3 Pro), Nano Banana Pro image generation, Veo video generation, raw exec — filling capability gaps Claude Code cannot meet alone.

plugins.name: contexthub
plugins.description: Four-phase Delphi debate primitive: Claude, OpenAI Codex, and Google Antigravity answer independently, critique each other, refine, then converge — Claude synthesizes the final answer with consensus and remaining disagreement.

plugins.name: triage
plugins.description: Token-efficient task grading: decomposes a task into single-or-subtask, grades each low/medium/hard, and writes a routing plan that the codex/agy/contexthub bridges read to set effort and model. The token-efficiency primitive — spend big effort only on hard work.

plugins.name: forge
plugins.description: The multi-agent software lifecycle: spec, plan, tdd, review, verify, debug, ship. Seven phases, each routing to the strongest specialist (Claude, Codex, Antigravity, contexthub) and graded for difficulty by triage. What Superpowers does, with three minds.
